rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign self-limiting pityriasis rosea — routine reassurance and symptomatic care with a safety-net for palm/sole/mucosal involvement (secondary syphilis). Not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rash spreads to the palms/soles, mouth sores appear, or the patient becomes systemically unwell with fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Would prompt secondary-syphilis serology or reassessment of the diagnosis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the rash reaches your palms or soles, you get mouth sores, or you feel unwell with a fever, see your GP — a blood test may be needed.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear written reassurance that it is not contagious and a specific safety-net.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is pityriasis rosea — a harmless, non-contagious post-viral rash that clears over 6–8 weeks. Use lukewarm showers and the anti-itch cream/antihistamine for comfort. See your GP if it reaches your palms/soles, you get mouth sores, or you feel unwell.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Routine GP in 2-3 weeks; sooner (serology) if palms/soles/mucosa involved
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless palm/sole/mucosal involvement or systemic illness.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document pityriasis rosea (secondary syphilis/drug eruption screened negative), symptomatic care, and a serology safety-net if atypical.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without screening for secondary syphilis or a drug eruption.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Secondary syphilis (or a severe drug eruption) is mislabelled as benign PR and goes untreated.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No itch management or safety-net, leaving the patient anxious/uncomfortable.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED referral for benign PR as a 'severe allergic reaction'.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine mucosa/nodes or do serology remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure after a palm/sole/mucosal and drug screen, and keep a serology safety-net for atypical features.
